preSessionState.CLAUDE.md:
# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing

{"entry_type":"decision","summary":"Notification system uses event-driven architecture via EventBus exclusively","detail":"EventBus is already the preferred pattern per CLAUDE.md, provides event logging/replay, supports typed events, and enables loose coupling. All notifications flow through EventBus.emit() → NotificationService listens → dispatches to NotificationAdapter implementations. The CallbackRegistry usage for order:status-changed will be migrated to EventBus.","tags":["architecture"],"scope":"src/services/notification.service.ts","agent_id":"agent-1","id":"01KN14X383G0JX2NWQ0WYZW0R6","timestamp":"2026-03-31T05:12:17.411Z"}
{"entry_type":"status","summary":"Agent 1: Notification system foundation complete — event-driven via EventBus, adapter pattern for channels","detail":"Implemented the notification system foundation with these components:

**Architecture: Event-driven via EventBus (exclusively)**
- Removed mixed CallbackRegistry pattern — all notifications now flow through EventBus
- NotificationService subscribes to events and dispatches to NotificationAdapter implementations

**New files:**
- `src/models/notification.ts` — Core types: NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationPriority, Notification
- `src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts` — NotificationAdapter interface (all channels must implement this)
- `src/adapters/email.adapter.ts` — EmailAdapter stub implementing NotificationAdapter

**Modified files:**
- `src/services/notification.service.ts` — Rewritten to use only EventBus + adapter dispatch via registerAdapter()
- `src/services/order.service.ts` — Migrated status-changed from CallbackRegistry to EventBus
- `src/index.ts` — Exports new types and adapters
- Tests updated to match

**Constraints for other developers:**
1. ALL notifications must go through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls
2. ALL delivery channels must implement NotificationAdapter interface
3. Register adapters via NotificationService.registerAdapter()
4. To add a new trigger: subscribe to event in registerEventHandlers()
5. To add a new channel: implement NotificationAdapter, register with service

All 70 tests pass, TypeScript compiles clean.","tags":["architecture","notification","event-driven"],"scope":"src/","agent_id":"agent-1","id":"01KN151BB5A5DKMPPX90093MYM","timestamp":"2026-03-31T05:14:36.773Z"}
